tasks.title: Harden dataset identity and reproducibility boundaries
tasks.description: Make each run point to a stable dataset or sync-checkpoint identity, close the remaining reproducibility gap called out in current-state, and extend market-data lineage or status so rerun equivalence can be validated without shell-only inspection.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 74e167c, which pinned dataset identity and reproducibility state into run provenance, surfaced it in UI and docs, and closed the top data-trust reproducibility gap.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly satisfies the data-trust direction's first success criterion and unblocks follow-up work on operator-visible market-data health and dataset-based rerun workflows.

tasks.title: Expose sync checkpoints and failure history in market-data status
tasks.description: Extend the market-data status surface so operators can inspect the latest sync checkpoint, freshness boundary, and recent failure history without shell access, closing the remaining visibility gap called out after dataset identity hardening.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 18a6f4c, which added checkpoint and failure-history persistence, surfaced the data in the control-room status board, and locked the behavior with API and UI tests.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly advances the remaining data-trust requirement that freshness, gaps, and failure history be actionable from product surfaces rather than shell inspection.

tasks.title: Add durable experiment metadata for tags, presets, and comparison filters
tasks.description: Move beyond payload-centric run lookup by adding durable run metadata and query paths for dataset identity, tags, preset selection, benchmark family, and richer comparison or export support so experiment retrieval matches Program Phase 1 expectations.
tasks.priority_reason: Current-state lists Stage 2 experiment workflow completion as Immediate Next Priority 2, but backlog ordering keeps this behind dataset identity hardening.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Covers the experiment-os requirement that runs be filterable by dataset, preset, tag, benchmark family, and version through durable metadata.

tasks.title: Link run provenance directly to sync checkpoints and rerun boundaries
tasks.description: Extend run provenance so each run directly references or pins the market-data sync checkpoint now exposed in status, then use that stable boundary to drive explicit rerun equivalence instead of relying on indirect dataset reconstruction.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 68f913f, which added aggregate and per-symbol sync checkpoint lineage, persisted rerun boundary IDs and state across native/reference runs, and exposed rerun-boundary lookup plus UI visibility.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the provenance-linking gap between data-trust checkpoint identity and experiment-os rerun flows by making sync checkpoints and rerun boundaries first-class run metadata.

tasks.title: Add explicit rerun workflow from stored rerun boundaries
tasks.description: Introduce an explicit rerun endpoint or workflow that accepts a prior run or rerun_boundary_id, reconstructs the stored boundary directly, and launches native or reference reruns without relying on manual operator reconstruction of provenance fields.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit bf93673, which added rerun_backtest_from_boundary, a rerun-boundary backtest endpoint, UI wiring, provenance match-status capture, and API plus application coverage for the explicit workflow.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly advances the experiment-os requirement that native and reference runs share one reproducible experiment model by turning stored rerun boundaries into a first-class rerun operation.

tasks.title: Extend rerun-boundary workflow beyond backtest-only execution
tasks.description: Broaden the new explicit rerun-boundary flow so operators can relaunch the other supported experiment lanes from stored boundaries, not just backtests, while preserving shared provenance fields and match or drift reporting across run types.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 08444e0, which introduced shared rerun-boundary execution for sandbox and paper aliases, added API and UI entry points, documented the workflow, and locked same-mode and cross-mode provenance behavior with tests.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps the experiment model unified across native and reference lanes by extending stored-boundary reruns beyond the first backtest-only surface.

tasks.title: Surface paper rerun-boundary replay as an explicit operator action
tasks.description: Extend the current UI and operator-facing replay surfaces so stored rerun boundaries can be relaunched into paper as a first-class action, instead of keeping paper replay available only through the API alias layer.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit a9b1141, which exposed explicit paper rerun-boundary actions in the control-room UI, updated operator-facing replay wording, aligned current-state documentation, and verified with typecheck plus build.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the shared experiment model by making the new rerun-boundary replay lanes consistently accessible from operator-visible product surfaces rather than API-only aliases.

tasks.title: Separate paper replay history and filters from sandbox alias storage
tasks.description: Refactor run persistence and query surfaces so rerun-boundary paper replays are stored and filterable as an explicit paper lane rather than collapsing into the existing sandbox alias, giving operators durable paper-specific history, counts, and replay lookup semantics.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 5945969, which added RunMode.PAPER, split paper run history and filters from sandbox storage in the API and control room, updated docs, and verified with API pytest plus web typecheck and build.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Strengthens the shared experiment model by ensuring paper remains a first-class execution lane in persisted run history and operator filters rather than a hidden alias of sandbox.

tasks.title: Separate paper execution semantics from the sandbox preview engine
tasks.description: Stop treating paper as a storage-only distinction on top of the sandbox preview engine by introducing paper-specific runtime semantics, supervision, and operator-facing state boundaries, so paper becomes a real execution lane instead of another sandbox alias with different history filters.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 8642bdc, which split paper runtime priming from sandbox preview replay, introduced paper-specific startup notes and current-snapshot semantics, aligned operator wording, and verified the change with API pytest plus web typecheck and build.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly advanced runtime-ops by separating preview-style sandbox behavior from paper startup semantics, creating a cleaner base for the remaining worker-session, health, and supervision work.

tasks.title: Reconcile legacy paper runs that were stored under sandbox history
tasks.description: Decide how to handle pre-RunMode.PAPER replay history that was persisted as sandbox without mode metadata, then add the chosen migration, annotation, or operator-facing caveat so legacy paper runs do not silently disappear or remain misclassified after the storage split.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply explicitly calls out that old sandbox-stored paper runs cannot be auto-classified, but the correct migration or disclosure policy still needs operator-product judgment before it should be auto-executed.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Preserves experiment-os trust in run history by making the post-split paper lane honest about legacy ambiguity instead of relying on silent misclassification.

tasks.title: Attach paper replay audit trail and approval metadata to operator actions
tasks.description: Add paper-specific operator-event capture, approval or acknowledgement metadata, and control-room visibility for rerun-boundary paper actions so replay decisions leave a durable audit trail instead of only changing run history.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply explicitly calls out missing paper-specific audit trail and approval metadata, but the storage-model separation for paper history is the more immediate executable queue head.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances operator trust by making paper replay actions explainable, reviewable, and durable in operator-visible records rather than implicit UI-triggered side effects.

tasks.title: Model cross-mode reruns as explicit drift or matched translation
tasks.description: Decide whether rerun-boundary identity should remain mode-specific with explicit drift notes, or whether backtest-to-sandbox and backtest-to-paper replays need a first-class matched-translation model with new provenance semantics and operator wording.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply says current cross-mode drift behavior is intentional but leaves open whether matched translation should be modeled separately, so this remains a valid follow-up that still needs product and provenance-shape judgment.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Clarifies the experiment-os contract for how stored boundaries map across execution lanes, keeping provenance and operator interpretation coherent as replay support expands.

tasks.title: Make rerun-boundary lookup durable and indexable in SQL storage
tasks.description: Move rerun_boundary_id and related summary fields out of payload-only filtering into durable SQL summary columns or indexes so common rerun-boundary queries do not depend on in-memory scans as run volume grows.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply explicitly calls out payload-scan-based boundary lookup as remaining work, but scope expansion of the rerun flow is the more direct queue-head follow-up before storage optimization.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Supports the experiment-os goal of queryable experiment metadata by promoting rerun-boundary access from payload scans to durable storage paths.

tasks.title: Make strategy lifecycle and registration durable across restarts
tasks.description: Replace the current process-local strategy registration behavior with durable strategy records and lifecycle states such as draft, benchmark candidate, sandbox candidate, live candidate, and archived so promotion decisions become queryable product state instead of static metadata.
tasks.priority_reason: Gate 1 and Program Phase 1 still require durable lifecycle state, but the latest main-session reply surfaced more immediate rerun workflow and rerun-boundary storage follow-ups ahead of lifecycle work.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly targets the experiment-os success criterion that strategy registration and lifecycle state remain durable and queryable across restarts.

tasks.title: Replace sandbox preview semantics with worker session state, heartbeat, and recovery
tasks.description: Introduce a real worker or session model separate from preview runs, then add heartbeat, restart recovery, and operator-visible runtime state so sandbox stops being a replay preview and can satisfy Gate 2 semantics.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit eb5c543, which replaced sandbox preview semantics with supervised runtime sessions, heartbeat and recovery state, worker maintenance jobs, aligned UI wording, and verification across API pytest plus web typecheck and build.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the worker-session foundation gap for runtime-ops by separating runtime sessions from preview semantics and making heartbeat plus recovery operator-visible, which now enables the remaining continuous-execution and alerting follow-ups.

tasks.title: Turn sandbox worker sessions into continuous execution workers
tasks.description: Extend the new supervised sandbox session model into a true continuous execution worker that stays alive across ticks, drives decision cycles from live venue or feed updates, and surfaces recent decisions, lag, positions, and fills as active runtime state rather than a primed preview snapshot.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 4766075, which turned sandbox worker sessions into continuous candle-processing execution loops, persisted processed and seen candle progress for restart recovery, surfaced worker progress in the control room, and verified the behavior with API pytest plus web typecheck and build.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the runtime-ops follow-up from worker-session foundations by making sandbox workers continuously process new candles with recoverable progress state, which now leaves alerting, audit visibility, and guarded live semantics as the next gaps.

tasks.title: Replace replay-shaped paper launch fields with a paper-specific request surface
tasks.description: Stop reusing sandbox replay request fields such as replay_bars for paper launch priming, and define a paper-specific API contract and UI wording that matches current-snapshot session startup rather than replay semantics.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply explicitly calls out that paper launch still reuses replay_bars for its priming window, but worker-session semantics are the more immediate executable follow-up.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps runtime-ops and operator wording coherent by making the paper launch contract reflect paper-session semantics instead of lingering replay terminology.

tasks.title: Connect worker failures and stale runtime state to operator alerts and audit visibility
tasks.description: Build the operator-facing alert and event path that turns surfaced market-data failure history, stale-data conditions, worker failures, and promotion decisions into durable actionable records instead of passive status text.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 942d20b, which added operator visibility models, runtime-derived worker failure and stale-runtime alerts, audit events for sandbox worker lifecycle transitions, a dedicated operator visibility API, control-room alert and audit panels, and matching API plus UI documentation updates.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the worker-failure and stale-runtime visibility gap for operator trust by turning continuous-worker session state into operator-facing alerts and audit events, which now leaves durable alert storage or delivery and broader safety-domain coverage as the next follow-ups.

tasks.title: Persist live-path alert history and broaden operator delivery coverage
tasks.description: Move runtime-derived alerts and guarded-live audit events beyond local control-state visibility by adding durable alert history, broader live-path operator event coverage, and a clear delivery boundary for notifications or downstream incident handling, so guarded-live safety visibility survives restarts and expands beyond the control room audit trail alone.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply after commit ea06cb9 still explicitly calls out wider live-path alerting and durable operator audit or event delivery as remaining work, but broader venue-native channel or session continuation is the first numbered remainder and venue-native amend semantics still sit closer to active guarded-live supervision scope.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances operator trust by extending guarded-live audit and alert surfaces into durable history and notification-ready delivery boundaries, covering the broader live path instead of leaving safety visibility confined to the current guarded-live audit trail.

tasks.title: Establish audit, kill-switch, and reconciliation primitives before live candidacy
tasks.description: Build the safety substrate that blocks live-affecting actions unless audit capture, operator-event logging, kill-switch controls, and reconciliation flows are present, keeping live readiness separate from sandbox worker readiness.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 7033ec7, which added guarded-live control state, kill-switch engage or release flows, local reconciliation runs, guarded-live audit history, candidacy blocker surfaces, and matching API, UI, test, and documentation coverage.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the first guarded-live safety substrate by making kill-switch, guarded-live audit, and reconciliation first-class product flows, which now exposes venue-aware reconciliation and real live execution adapters as the remaining candidacy gaps.

tasks.title: Upgrade reconciliation from local control-plane checks to venue-state verification
tasks.description: Add the first venue-facing guarded-live read path for balances, positions, orders, or account state, then use it to reconcile local runtime and control-plane state against real venue snapshots so live candidacy gates are backed by external truth rather than local consistency alone.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 5f7b185, which added venue-state adapters, persisted internal and venue snapshots into reconciliation results, surfaced venue verification state in the control room, and verified the change with API pytest plus web typecheck and build.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the first venue-truth reconciliation gap for guarded-live execution by comparing local state against real venue balances and open orders, which now exposes restart recovery from venue snapshots and the eventual live write path as the remaining candidacy steps.

tasks.title: Recover guarded-live runtime state from venue snapshots after restart or fault
tasks.description: Extend guarded-live beyond snapshot comparison so restart or fault handling can rebuild or quarantine runtime state from venue balances and open orders, letting venue verification drive safe recovery instead of ending at mismatch reporting alone.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit aac2910, which rebuilt guarded-live runtime state from verified venue snapshots, exposed recovery state and a recovery action through the API and control room, persisted recovery audit details, and verified the change with API pytest plus web typecheck and build.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the restart-recovery gap in guarded-live execution by reconstructing persisted runtime state from verified venue snapshots, which now leaves real venue-backed worker execution and broader live-path audit or alert coverage as the next candidacy steps.

tasks.title: Introduce a guarded live worker and venue order path behind safety gates
tasks.description: Implement the first real venue-backed guarded-live worker loop and order submission or cancellation path behind the new safety controls so recovered runtime state can transition into supervised live execution instead of stopping at control-plane reconstruction alone, while keeping kill-switch, audit, and reconciliation preconditions mandatory for every live-affecting action.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 24914e2, which added a dedicated guarded-live worker session with heartbeat and recovery, a real venue execution port with seeded and Binance adapters, gated live start or stop APIs and UI, and market-order venue submission behind launch safety checks.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the first venue-backed guarded-live execution step by turning recovered runtime state into a supervised live worker and gated market-order path, which now leaves richer order lifecycle sync, fuller venue-session resumption, and broader alert coverage as the next candidacy gaps.

tasks.title: Extend guarded-live from market submit only to order lifecycle sync
tasks.description: Broaden the new guarded-live venue execution path beyond market-order submission by adding venue order-state synchronization so active live runs can reconcile and supervise open, partially filled, and completed venue orders instead of treating venue writes as fire-and-forget submissions.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 9a912e6, which added guarded-live venue order-state sync, periodic open-order reconciliation, local fills and positions progression, operator-facing lifecycle summaries, and matching API plus application coverage.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the first guarded-live order-lifecycle supervision gap by keeping local live state synced to venue order progression, which now leaves active order management controls and restart-safe durable venue session resume as the remaining candidacy steps.

tasks.title: Add guarded-live cancel or replace controls for active venue orders
tasks.description: Extend guarded-live beyond passive order-state syncing by adding explicit cancel or replace order-management flows, audit capture, and operator-facing controls for active venue orders so supervision can actively manage in-flight live exposure instead of only observing venue progression.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 4f02c04, which added guarded-live cancel and replace APIs, recovery-seeded active-order controls in the control room, audit-visible local order-state updates, and API plus application test coverage.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the guarded-live active-order supervision gap by letting operators cancel or reprice in-flight venue orders from recovered run state, which now leaves restart-safe live session ownership, durable order-book sync, and broader live-path alert coverage as the next candidacy steps.

tasks.title: Decide whether guarded-live replace must use venue-native amend semantics
tasks.description: Evaluate whether the current cancel-plus-new-limit replace flow is sufficient for guarded-live candidacy or whether venue-native atomic amend support is required, then implement the chosen contract with matching audit and operator wording if native amend semantics are mandatory.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply after commit ea06cb9 still explicitly calls out that live order management stops at cancel or replace and does not yet support venue-native amend semantics, but broader venue-native channel or session continuation remains the first direct executable follow-up from the completed Binance restore expansion.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps guarded-live candidacy explicit about whether order replacement semantics must match venue-native amend guarantees before live exposure management is considered complete.

tasks.title: Resume guarded-live with durable venue order-book sync and live session ownership
tasks.description: Upgrade guarded-live recovery and launch so restart or recovery does not just rebuild local state from snapshots, but durably syncs the venue order book and resumes or quarantines the live worker session with explicit ownership semantics for in-flight venue activity.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 8950ec9, which persisted guarded-live ownership and durable open-order snapshots, reattached the owned live run on resume, reseeded venue order state after restart or fault, exposed the ownership surface in the control room, and verified the flow with API pytest plus web typecheck and build.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the durable ownership and order-book snapshot resume gap for guarded-live execution, which now leaves venue-native session lifecycle restoration and broader live-path alert coverage as the next candidacy steps.

tasks.title: Restore guarded-live from venue-native session lifecycle, not snapshot reseeding alone
tasks.description: Extend guarded-live resume beyond persisted run state plus durable open-order snapshots so restart or fault recovery can reconcile and reattach against the venue's native session and exchange order lifecycle directly, including explicit quarantine behavior when a prior live session cannot be authoritatively resumed.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 044a40e, which restored tracked venue order lifecycle from venue-native session state ahead of snapshot fallback, persisted session_restore control state, surfaced restore provenance in the control room, and verified the resume path with API pytest plus web typecheck and build.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances guarded-live candidacy from restart-safe ownership and snapshot recovery toward authoritative venue-native session restoration, so live recovery no longer depends on local reseeding semantics alone.

tasks.title: Hand off a full venue-native stream/session lifecycle after guarded-live resume
tasks.description: Extend guarded-live resume beyond restoring tracked order lifecycle so the resumed owner reattaches to the venue-native stream or session lifecycle itself, keeps receiving authoritative venue events after restart, and quarantines clearly when continuous venue-native handoff cannot be re-established.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 21ff422, which persisted guarded-live session handoff state, added venue session handoff, sync, and release contracts, kept the live worker attached to the owned venue session lifecycle after resume, surfaced handoff details in the control room, and verified the change with API pytest plus web typecheck and build.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the guarded-live venue-session handoff gap by persisting session ownership across resume and live maintenance, which now leaves venue-push session transport, amend semantics, and broader live-path alert coverage as the remaining candidacy gaps.

tasks.title: Replace the Binance handoff bridge with a venue-push websocket session stream
tasks.description: Advance the new persisted guarded-live session handoff beyond sync-based bridging so Binance live supervision owns a venue-push websocket session or equivalent push transport, keeps order lifecycle updates authoritative without polling-style maintenance, and quarantines clearly when push delivery drops or cannot be resumed.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit ebd91e4, which replaced the Binance handoff bridge with a websocket-backed user-data session stream, drained pushed executionReport events into local order state, added stream lifecycle tests, and verified the change with API pytest plus web typecheck and build before push.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances guarded-live candidacy from persisted session ownership to authoritative venue-push supervision, reducing reliance on sync loops and making live session continuity match the venue transport model more directly.

tasks.title: Extend Binance push-session supervision to failover and broader venue-native coverage
tasks.description: Build the next guarded-live step after the new Binance websocket user-data stream by adding explicit session failover or reattach behavior and widening venue-native push coverage beyond the current order-lifecycle-only transport, so live supervision degrades or quarantines cleanly when the push stream drops and can grow toward fuller venue session scope without returning to polling bridges.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 6f735dd, which added Binance user-data session failover with listen-key reattachment, expanded supervision coverage to outboundAccountPosition, balanceUpdate, and listStatus alongside executionReport, surfaced the richer session state in the control room, and verified with API pytest plus web typecheck and build before push.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the first Binance push-session resilience step by adding failover and wider user-data event coverage, which now leaves market-depth or broader venue-native session transport, amend semantics, and wider live-path alert delivery as the remaining candidacy gaps.

tasks.title: Broaden guarded-live venue-native session transport beyond Binance user-data events
tasks.description: Extend the guarded-live venue-native supervision model beyond the current Binance user-data account and order event coverage by deciding and implementing the next authoritative transport surface, such as market-depth or broader venue-native push channels, so live supervision can track more of the venue session without falling back to polling-style gaps.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit a528cd4, which broadened guarded-live venue transport from Binance user-data only to a combined multi-stream websocket carrying user-data plus trade and book-ticker events, added full-session failover, surfaced the richer handoff state in the control room, and verified with API pytest plus web typecheck and build before push.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the next Binance venue-session transport step by folding market trade and book-ticker streams into the guarded-live handoff, which now leaves depth or order-book, kline, wider venue-native session breadth, amend semantics, and durable alert delivery as the remaining candidacy gaps.

tasks.title: Extend guarded-live venue session coverage to depth and the next market-data channels
tasks.description: Build on the new Binance multi-stream venue session by deciding and implementing the next coverage layer after trade and book-ticker, starting with authoritative depth or order-book transport and any immediately adjacent kline or venue-native market channels needed for guarded-live supervision, so the live session model keeps expanding without dropping back to polling-style gaps.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit 8d7b77b, which widened the Binance multi-stream handoff to depthUpdate and timeframe-aware kline coverage, persisted depth or kline timing in session handoff state, surfaced the new timestamps in the control room, and verified the behavior with API pytest plus web typecheck and build before push.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the next Binance venue-session transport gap by extending the guarded-live handoff through depth and timeframe-aware kline coverage, which now leaves wider venue-native channel breadth and a fuller order-book lifecycle as the next explicit live-session supervision gap.

tasks.title: Extend guarded-live supervision to fuller order-book lifecycle and wider venue-native channels
tasks.description: Build on the completed trade, book-ticker, depth, and timeframe-aware kline handoff by adding the next executable layer of guarded-live venue supervision: fuller order-book lifecycle coverage and the next venue-native market-data channels that close the remaining live-session blind spots without falling back to polling-style recovery.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit b3f6ee8, which widened Binance guarded-live supervision to aggTrade and miniTicker, persisted order_book_state plus depth gap and top-of-book handoff fields, surfaced the richer state in the control room, and verified with API pytest plus web typecheck and build before push.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the next guarded-live supervision gap by extending venue-native coverage through aggTrade, miniTicker, and persisted order-book continuity signals, which now leaves full depth snapshot bridging or local book rebuild, wider exchange transport, and venue-native amend plus alert delivery as the next candidacy gaps.

tasks.title: Bridge guarded-live failover through full depth snapshots and local book rebuild
tasks.description: Turn the new `resync_required` order-book supervision signal into executable guarded-live recovery by fetching or bridging a full depth snapshot after failover, rebuilding the local book against update IDs, and quarantining clearly when authoritative order-book continuity cannot be restored.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit bacc33d, which bridged guarded-live failover through venue depth snapshots and local book rebuild, persisted rebuild counters and snapshot metadata in session handoff state, surfaced the rebuild details in the control room, and verified the recovery path with API pytest plus web typecheck and build.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the guarded-live depth recovery gap by turning failover and depth-gap resync into authoritative snapshot-backed local book rebuilds, which now leaves fuller venue-native order-book continuation, deeper market-channel restoration, amend semantics, and durable live-path alert delivery as the next candidacy steps.

tasks.title: Continue guarded-live with full venue order-book recovery and deeper channel restore
tasks.description: Build on the completed snapshot bridge and local book rebuild by restoring the next authoritative live-session layer: full venue-native order-book continuation after rebuild and the deeper market-data channels still missing from the guarded-live handoff, so recovery does not stop at reseeding a local book when the venue session should keep streaming forward.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed in main session commit ea06cb9, which extended guarded-live resume through recovered bid or ask ladders plus channel-restore state, rebuilt the local book from full depth snapshot or persisted handoff across failover and restart, restored deeper ticker, trades, and ohlcv channels without overwriting rebuilt top-of-book state, surfaced the richer handoff in the control room, and verified with API pytest plus web typecheck and build before push.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the next guarded-live restore gap by carrying full order-book recovery through deeper ticker, trades, and ohlcv channel restoration, which now leaves broader venue-native channel or session continuation beyond the current Binance-focused restore, plus amend semantics and durable live-path alert delivery, as the next candidacy steps.

tasks.title: Broaden guarded-live continuation beyond the current Binance channel restore
tasks.description: Build on the completed full order-book recovery and deeper channel restore by extending guarded-live recovery beyond the current Binance multi-stream plus exchange-backed ticker, trades, and ohlcv restore, deciding and implementing the next venue-native channel or session continuation layer so resumed live supervision does not stop at adapter-specific reseeding.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply after commit ea06cb9 explicitly says the restore now covers Binance multi-stream plus exchange-backed ticker, trades, and ohlcv recovery, but broader venue-native channel or session continuation is still the first remaining item, making it the clearest next executable queue head.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances guarded-live candidacy from a restored Binance-specific handoff toward a broader venue-native continuation model, keeping restart recovery focused on authoritative channel or session continuity before lower-priority amend and alerting follow-ups.

tasks.title: Keep the LLM lane isolated until trace, replay, and fallback are real features
tasks.description: Implement the minimum research-lane substrate for prompt versioning, raw trace storage, replay or evaluation hooks, and mandatory deterministic fallback or human review so DecisionEnginePort stops being only an interface skeleton.
tasks.priority_reason: Current-state lists LLM isolation with trace storage, replay, and fallback as Immediate Next Priority 5, but the roadmap treats it as downstream of research-core and runtime-operability foundations.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Matches the intelligence-research-lane success criteria that every intelligence run be traceable and blocked from promotion without fallback or review.
